Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Citizenship Act (Oath of Citizenship)
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Citizenship Act (Oath of Citizenship)

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Citizenship Act to provide two options for the oath of citizenship.
His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: Section 24 of the Citizenship Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Requirement to take oath of citizenship
Section.Text: If a person is required under this Act to take the oath of citizenship, the person shall, in accordance with the regulations, swear or affirm their citizenship in the form set out in either paragraph (a) or (b):

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: I swear (or affirm) that I will be faithful and bear true allegiance to His Majesty King Charles III, King of Canada, His Heirs and Successors, and that I will faithfully observe the laws of Canada, including the Constitution, which recognizes and affirms the Aboriginal and treaty rights of First Nations, Inuit and MÃ©tis peoples, and fulfil my duties as a Canadian citizen;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: I swear (or affirm) that I will be faithful and bear true allegiance to Canada and that I will faithfully observe the laws of Canada, including the Constitution, which recognizes and affirms the Aboriginal and treaty rights of First Nations, Inuit and MÃ©tis peoples, and fulfil my duties as a Canadian citizen.

Section.Text: Where a person is required under this Act to take the oath of citizenship, the person shall swear or affirm in the form set out in the schedule and in accordance with the regulations.
The Schedule to the Act is repealed.

Provision.Oath: I swear (or affirm) that I will be faithful and bear true allegiance to Her Majesty Queen Elizabeth the Second, Queen of Canada, Her Heirs and Successors, and that I will faithfully observe the laws of Canada, including the Constitution, which recognizes and affirms the Aboriginal and treaty rights of First Nations, Inuit and MÃ©tis peoples, and fulfil my duties as a Canadian citizen.

Section.Text: This Act comes into force on the day after the day on which it receives royal assent.
